ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers tofu soup as a category; no specific chrysanthemum tofu soup code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of chrysanthemum tofu soup is approximately 350 g, comprising silken tofu carved into chrysanthemum shapes served in a mushroom-based broth.

nutrient_source.title: Tofu, soft, prepared with calcium sulfate — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 16127); Mushroom broth estimated from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods mushroom soup components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for soft tofu (NDB 16127) combined with a mushroom broth component estimate. The dish is approximately 55% silken tofu and 45% mushroom broth by weight. Weighted composite per-100g values are calculated accordingly. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry for chrysanthemum tofu soup was found; USDA SR Legacy is the highest-priority approved source available for this dish type.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chrysanthemum tofu soup. GI estimated at 15 based on the GI of soft tofu (GI ~15, low GI food per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The dish is predominantly tofu in a low-carbohydrate mushroom broth; the broth contributes negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (15 × 6.3) / 100 ≈ 0.9, where carbohydrate per 350g serving = 1.8 × 3.5 = 6.3g.

hypertension.guidance: Each 350g bowl provides approximately 630mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth or limit portion size to manage daily sodium intake below 2000mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 15 and only 6.3g of carbohydrate per serving, this soup has a negligible glycemic load of 0.9 and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as part of a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and soy isoflavone content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Silken tofu provides soy isoflavones and polyunsaturated fats with zero cholesterol and low saturated fat (0.2g per 100g), supporting a heart-healthy dietary pattern; sodium from broth should be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chrysanthemum Tofu Soup (菊花豆腐汤, Júhuā dòufu tāng) is a refined Chinese soup dish in which silken tofu is hand-carved into the shape of chrysanthemum flowers and served in a delicate, umami-rich mushroom broth. The dish is a specialty of contemporary Chinese cuisine and is particularly associated with artisanal restaurant cooking in Singapore and mainland China. The preparation involves scoring soft tofu blocks with fine cuts to create petal-like formations that bloom when submerged in hot broth, demonstrating skilled knife craftsmanship. The broth is typically made from dried shiitake or other mushrooms, seasoned lightly with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, and spring onion, producing a clean, earthy, and subtly savory flavor profile. The texture of the tofu is silky and delicate, contrasting with the clear, aromatic broth. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories (approximately 28 kcal per 100g), low in carbohydrates (1.8g per 100g), and provides moderate plant-based protein (2.4g per 100g) from tofu. It is naturally cholesterol-free and contains soy isoflavones, which have been associated with cardiovascular benefits. The primary nutritional concern is sodium from the broth, which can reach 630mg per bowl. The glycemic index is very low (estimated GI ~15), making it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable. It is commonly served as a starter or light soup course in Chinese banquet or restaurant settings.
common_user_queries: Is tofu soup good for diabetics?
How many calories in tofu soup?
What mushrooms are used in tofu soup?
Is tofu soup high in sodium?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for chrysanthemum tofu soup. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy soft tofu data combined with a mushroom broth component estimate. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database tofu reference. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate: approximately 55% silken tofu (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 16127) and 45% mushroom broth (estimated from USDA mushroom soup components). trans_fat_g is 0.0 as tofu and mushroom broth contain no industrially produced trans fats (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as tofu is a plant-based food containing no cholesterol (naturally zero). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients (naturally zero). vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source for soft tofu and mushroom broth components. Sodium content is notable at approximately 180mg per 100g (630mg per 350g serving) due to seasoned mushroom broth; individuals with hypertension should be advised accordingly. GI estimated at 15 by analogy with soft tofu per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in approved sources. FoodOn URI FOODON_03311434 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are used as closest parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for chrysanthemum-carved tofu soup. The dish is served at See Ya Tonight 今晚有局 restaurant in Singapore at SGD 15.60.
